LAW AND MOTION 302, DEFENDANT AARON BLACKLEDGE'S DEMURRER TO 1ST AMENDED COMPLAINT. NO APPEARANCE ON THIS MATTER. THE COURT ADOPTED ITS TENTATIVE RULING - SUSTAINED WITH TEN DAYS LEAVE TO AMEND. PLAINTIFF HAS NOT PLED FACTS SUFFICIENT TO ALLEGE THAT THE DEFENDANT KNEW, OR SHOULD HAVE KNOWN, THAT THE DRIVER WAS INCOMPETENT OR UNFIT TO DRIVE THE VEHICLE, AND IS NOW GIVEN LEAVE TO DO SO, IF IT CAN DO SO IN GOOD FAITH. (PREVAILING PARTY TO PREPARE A FORM OF ORDER.) JUDGE: HAROLD KAHN, REPORTER: TEANNA WARD, CSR # 11918

DEMURRER TO 1ST AMENDED COMPLAINT
Off Calendar Jan-19-2012 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:

No appearance on this matter. The Court adopted its tentative ruling -
DEFENDANT AARON BLACKLEDGE'S DEMURRER TO 1ST AMENDED COMPLAINT is Sustained with ten days leave to amend. Plaintiff has not pled facts sufficient to allege that the defendant knew, or should have known, that the driver was incompetent or unfit to drive the vehicle, and is now given leave to do so, if it can do so in good faith. (Prevailing party to prepare a form of order.) Judge: Harold Kahn, Reporter: Teanna Ward, CSR # 11918 =(302/HEK)
